title: Phase 2 Integration: Geometry, Mesh, and Solver Unification

learning_objectives.topic: Phase 2 Geometry & Mesh components into the Phase 1 solver
learning_objectives.importance: Unify the foundation (Phase 1) with the geometry pipeline (Phase 2) to create a complete, end-to-end CFD engine.

learning_objectives.topic: Automatic mesh quality control and adaptive refinement triggers
learning_objectives.challenge: Linking cell-level field data (alpha, grad(T)) to dynamic mesh operations.

learning_objectives.topic: Unified solver architecture that reads geometry, builds mesh, and solves physics

theory_sections.title_th: สถาปัตยกรรมของ Solver ที่บูรณาการ

equations.name: Mesh Quality Metric
equations.explanation: Combined metric for non-orthogonality and skewness, used to trigger local refinement.

equations.name: Refinement Criterion (Interface)
equations.explanation: Trigger refinement where the alpha gradient is high (interface region).

equations.name: Refinement Criterion (Thermal)
equations.explanation: Trigger refinement where the temperature gradient is high (thermal boundary layer).

variables.name: Cell Quality Factor
variables.description: > 5 indicates poor quality, triggers mesh smoothing.

variables.name: Refinement Indicator
variables.description: Binary (0/1) flag to split a cell based on field criteria.

theory_sections.warning: Dynamic mesh refinement changes the LDU addressing during runtime. Solvers must be able to update field connectivity and restart matrix assembly.

theory_sections.title_th: การไหลของข้อมูลจากเรขาคณิตสู่ตัวแก้ปัญหา

equations.name: Boundary Condition Mapping
equations.explanation: Mapping function that assigns CAD region names (e.g., 'inlet_pipe', 'heater_wall') to OpenFOAM boundary types (e.g., 'fixedValue', 'zeroGradient').

variables.name: Mapping Dictionary
variables.description: A runtime dictionary (e.g., `boundaryMapping`) that links geometric entities to physics.

theory_sections.warning: Incorrect BC mapping is a silent error. A wall might accidentally get an inlet condition, causing mass imbalance and divergence.

openfoam_analysis.purpose: The core polyhedral mesh class, now fully constructed from Phase 2 operations (snappyHexMesh, refinement).

key_members.name: points_
key_members.purpose: List of all vertex coordinates. Updated after refinement.

key_members.name: faces_
key_members.purpose: List of faces. Connectivity changes after dynamic refinement.

key_members.name: owner_
key_members.purpose: Owner cell for each face. Rebuilt after mesh changes.

key_members.name: neighbour_
key_members.purpose: Neighbour cell for each internal face. Rebuilt after mesh changes.

key_members.name: boundary_
key_members.purpose: List of boundary patches. Must be preserved during refinement.

key_methods.name: updateMesh(const mapPolyMesh& mpm)
key_methods.purpose: CRITICAL: Updates all mesh-dependent data after refinement. Called by mesh modifiers.

key_methods.name: globalData()
key_methods.purpose: Returns processor communication info (important for parallel runs).

openfoam_analysis.purpose: Abstract base class for meshes that change over time. The bridge between Phase 2 mesh tools and the Phase 1 solver.

key_members.name: dynamicMeshDict_
key_members.purpose: Dictionary containing refinement criteria (e.g., based on 'alpha', 'grad(T)').

key_methods.name: update()
key_methods.purpose: Pure virtual function. Performs mesh modification (refinement/unrefinement) and returns a map of changes.

key_methods.name: refine(const word& fieldName, const scalar lowerRefineLevel, ...)
key_methods.purpose: Refines cells where the field value meets criteria. Must be called before the solver step.

openfoam_analysis.critical_note: The solver loop must call `mesh.update()` and then map all existing fields (U, p, T, alpha) to the new mesh topology before proceeding with the next time step.

openfoam_analysis.purpose: Finite Volume mesh, now inheriting from dynamicFvMesh for integrated operation.

key_members.name: schemesDict_
key_members.purpose: Discretization schemes. Must be re-read if mesh topology changes drastically?

key_methods.name: C()
key_methods.purpose: Returns cell centers. Must be recalculated after refinement.

openfoam_analysis.critical_note: In the integrated solver, `fvMesh` is constructed via `MeshManager` which reads the `blockMeshDict` and executes `snappyHexMesh`.

classes.name: IntegratedEvaporatorSolver
classes.responsibility: The master solver class that orchestrates the entire simulation from geometry to solution.

key_members.name: meshManager_
key_members.purpose: Handles all geometry and mesh operations (Phase 2).

key_members.name: physicsSolver_
key_members.purpose: Handles the PDE solution (U, p, T, alpha) from Phase 1.

key_members.name: refinementTrigger_
key_members.purpose: Strategy pattern for deciding when/where to refine based on field data.

key_methods.name: readGeometryAndMesh(const fileName& cadFile)
key_methods.purpose: Entry point. Calls MeshManager to import CAD, run blockMesh, snappyHexMesh, and initial refinement.

key_methods.name: solveTimeStep()
key_methods.purpose: Main loop. 1) Check refinement criteria. 2) Update mesh if needed. 3) Map fields. 4) Execute Phase 1 PISO/VOF/Energy solve.

key_methods.name: mapFieldsAfterRefinement(const mapPolyMesh&)
key_methods.purpose: CRITICAL: Uses the polyTopoChangeMap to interpolate all volFields and surfaceFields to the new mesh.

classes.critical_implementation: The solveTimeStep() loop must handle the conditional logic: `if (timeToRefine) { updateMesh(); mapFields(); }`. Field mapping must conserve integral properties (mass, energy).

classes.name: MeshManager
classes.responsibility: Manages the lifecycle of the mesh from CAD to final dynamic mesh.

key_methods.name: createBaseMesh(const dictionary& blockMeshDict)
key_methods.purpose: Executes blockMesh to create the background hex mesh.

key_methods.name: snapToGeometry(const fileName& stlFile, const dictionary& snapDict)
key_methods.purpose: Executes snappyHexMesh to conform the mesh to STL surfaces and add boundary layers.

key_methods.name: attachDynamicRefiner(const dictionary& refineDict)
key_methods.purpose: Attaches a dynamicRefineFvMesh object to the mesh for runtime control.

classes.name: RefinementCriterion
classes.responsibility: Abstract base class for evaluating if a cell should be refined.

key_methods.name: refineFlag(const volScalarField& field)
key_methods.purpose: Returns a labelList of cell IDs to refine based on the field (e.g., alpha, gradT).

algorithm_steps: e. Initialize fields (U, p, T, alpha) on the final mesh.
a. Calculate CFL, adjust time step.
d. **Physics Solve:** Execute Phase1SolverCore::solve(U, p, T, alpha, mDot).
e. Write output, update time, loop.

concept_checks.question: หลังจาก Dynamic Mesh Refinement, ทำไมต้องทำการ map fields (เช่น U, p) ไปยัง mesh ใหม่? และวิธี map ที่ถูกต้องคืออะไร?
answer_key_points: Refinement สร้างเซลล์ใหม่และลบเซลล์เก่าออก โครงสร้าง addressing (owner, neighbour) เปลี่ยนทั้งหมด
Field values ในเซลล์เก่าต้องถูก interpolate ไปยังเซลล์ใหม่เพื่อเป็น initial condition สำหรับขั้นตอนถัดไป
OpenFOAM ใช้ `mapPolyMesh` object ซึ่งเก็บ mapping ระหว่าง old cell/face IDs กับ new IDs
Method ที่ถูกต้อง: สร้าง field ใหม่โดยใช้ constructor `volScalarField alphaNew(alpha, meshMap)` ซึ่งจะทำ interpolation อัตโนมัติ
ต้อง map ทุก field: volScalarField, volVectorField, surfaceScalarField (phi)

concept_checks.question: การ Refinement แบบ Dynamic ในขณะที่ Solver กำลังทำงาน (Runtime) ส่งผลกระทบต่อขั้นตอน Pressure-Velocity Coupling (PISO) อย่างไร?
answer_key_points: Matrix Coefficients (A in AU = H - grad(p)) เปลี่ยนไปเพราะ cell volume (V_p) และ face areas (S_f) เปลี่ยน
ต้อง reassemble matrix ใหม่ทั้งหมดหลังจาก refinement เสร็จสิ้น ก่อน PISO loop ถัดไป
Face flux field (phi) ต้องถูก map/interpolate อย่างถูกต้อง เพราะใช้ใน divergence terms
Rhie-Chow interpolation coefficients (1/A_p) ต้องคำนวณใหม่จาก matrix ใหม่
ในทางปฏิบัติ: หลังจาก mapFields แล้ว ให้เริ่ม PISO loop ใหม่โดยสมบูรณ์ (ไม่ใช่ต่อจาก iteration ก่อนหน้า)
concept_checks.implementation_note: ใน `solveTimeStep()`, เรียก `physicsSolver_->solve()` ใหม่ทั้งหมดหลัง refinement

pitfalls.symptom: Simulation crashes with error 'field size does not match mesh size' after refinement.
pitfalls.cause: Fields were not mapped to the new mesh. The old field object still has the old size (nOldCells), but the solver is trying to use it on a mesh with nNewCells.
pitfalls.fix: Ensure `mapFieldsAfterRefinement` is called for ALL vol and surface fields. Use the field constructor that takes a `mapPolyMesh` argument.

pitfalls.symptom: Mass or energy is not conserved after mesh refinement/unrefinement.
pitfalls.cause: Naive interpolation during field mapping does not conserve integrals. For example, linearly interpolating alpha does not guarantee the total volume of liquid is the same.
pitfalls.fix: Implement conservative mapping. For scalar α, calculate total volume V_liquid = sum(α_i * V_i) on old mesh. After mapping, scale the new α field to preserve V_liquid.

pitfalls.symptom: Solver becomes very slow after several refinement steps.
pitfalls.cause: Refinement increases cell count globally without unrefinement in regions where it's no longer needed (e.g., where the interface has moved away).
pitfalls.fix: Implement two-level refinement criteria in `dynamicMeshDict`: `refine` AND `unrefine`. Unrefine cells where the field gradient falls below a threshold.

connections.next_day: Day 20: Phase 3 Preview - Turbulence & Advanced Models
connections.key_link: Today's integrated solver uses the dynamic mesh from Day 18 and the core physics from Phase 1 (Days 1-12) to create a fully functional evaporator simulator. This is the culmination of Phase 2.
